title: euphrasia parviflora subsp. glabrescens
intro: Euphrasia parviflora subsp. glabrescens, also known as the small-flowered eyebright, is a subspecies of the Euphrasia parviflora plant, a flowering perennial found in Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids, glycosides, and iridoid compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Euphrasia parviflora subsp. glabrescens include its ability to soothe eye irritations, reduce inflammation, and support respiratory health, making it a popular herbal remedy for allergies and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried herb or made into teas, tinctures, and capsules, with some practitioners also recommending it as a topical eye treatment for conjunctivitis and other eye infections.
